name: CircularProgress
description: A compact, inline "working" spinner — used where a linear bar doesn't fit (inside a button, next to a small control, or as a self-contained loading indicator). Split out of the old repo's single umbrella "ProgressIndicator" component: the real library has two separate components, CircularProgress and LinearProgress, not one component with a type prop switching between them. karoo-ui's wrapper is a bare re-export of MUI's <CircularProgress> — no overrides.
keyRule: Use CircularProgress for a compact/inline indicator (inside a button, a card, a small region). Use determinate when you know the percentage; indeterminate (the default) when you don't. For content loading into a known layout, prefer a skeleton over a spinner.

variants.name: variant="indeterminate" (default)
variants.whenToUse: A small inline "working" spinner where duration is unknown.

variants.name: variant="determinate"
variants.whenToUse: A compact, known-percentage indicator — pair with `value` (0-100).

anatomy.name: Track/arc
anatomy.description: the circular stroke; for determinate it fills proportionally to `value`, for indeterminate it animates continuously.

behaviors.name: Determinate vs indeterminate
behaviors.description: Switch to variant="determinate" as soon as a real percentage is available via `value`; don't fake progress. Stays indeterminate (the default) otherwise.

behaviors.name: Delay
behaviors.description: For fast operations, delay showing a spinner briefly to avoid a flicker — this is caller-side logic, not a CircularProgress prop.

behaviors.name: Skeletons
behaviors.description: When loading into a known structure (a card, a row), a skeleton placeholder feels faster and less jarring than a spinner.

states.treatment: Continuous rotate animation; disableShrink=false (default) also animates arc length.
Arc length reflects `value`; no rotation animation.
Caller hides the component, or briefly shows value=100 before dismissing — not a built-in state.

specs.value: 40px (MUI unoverridden default) — pass `size` (number = px, or a CSS length string) for other sizes.
primary (semantic.color.brand.primary.main, #F47735) — color="primary" is MUI's own default.

props.name: variant
props.description: Determinate shows a known percentage via `value`; indeterminate animates for unknown duration.

props.name: size
props.description: Numbers are px; strings must include a CSS unit (e.g. '3rem').

props.name: color
props.description: Theme colour applied to the arc.

props.name: disableShrink
props.description: Disables the shrink animation; only affects the 'indeterminate' variant.

props.name: enableTrackSlot
props.description: Mounts a track circle slot behind the progress arc for a subtle background — `size`/`thickness` apply to both.

doThis: Use determinate progress when you know the percentage.
Prefer skeletons for content loads into a known layout.
Delay spinners on fast (sub-second) operations to avoid flicker.
Give it an accessible label via aria-label or a visually adjacent label — CircularProgress itself renders no text.
dontDoThis: Don't block the whole screen for a small async action — scope the spinner to the region that's actually loading.
Don't reach for a `type` prop to pick circular vs linear — they're two separate components now; import LinearProgress directly for the bar shape.
accessibility: Renders role="progressbar" with aria-valuenow set automatically for determinate; indeterminate omits aria-valuenow per ARIA spec.
Give it an accessible label (aria-label or aria-labelledby) describing what's loading — MUI doesn't add one by default.
Don't rely on motion alone; pair with visible text where the loading state matters to the task.

related.name: LinearProgress
related.why: The bar-shaped sibling — use for a region-width indicator instead of an inline spinner

related.name: Snackbar
related.why: Announce completion when the wait ends

related.name: DataGrid
related.why: Its `loading` prop renders its own overlay spinner rather than composing CircularProgress directly
